name: Turnip

pros.title: lighter root option with a low calorie load
pros.explanation: That gives turnip a different kind of value than denser staple tubers.

pros.title: gives useful vitamin C for the calories
pros.explanation: It is not a nutrition monster overall, but this is a fair specific strength.

pros.title: clean whole-food flexibility
pros.explanation: It fits a lot of simple cooking uses without needing heavy rescue from processing.

cons.title: not a strong staple-carb performer
cons.explanation: Compared with stronger tubers, it is less convincing as a main energy anchor.

cons.title: fibre return is only modest
cons.explanation: Enough to count, not enough to clearly separate it upward.

cons.title: micronutrient profile is respectable rather than standout
cons.explanation: Useful, but not strong enough to make turnip feel like a category leader.

sourceNotes: Expanded dataset sample for FoodRanked pressure-testing.
Values are approximate representative placeholders for category tuning, not a clinical nutrient database.
